highlights.text: In this regard, I trade 1 setup: The Failed Breakdown, which is the price footprint of institutional accumulation.
Failed Breakdowns occur as the 2nd part of a two part sequence I call the two siblings.
The first part is price goes elevator down and flushes every nearby support.
I wrote last Tuesday October 21st at 4pm (over one week ago): “The best setup here is to wait for the Failed Breakdown of Sunday’s 6694 daily low.
We flushed 90 points down to 6690, Failed Breakdown, recovered 6694 and the leg up began which then broke us out the triangle.
After FOMC yesterday the cycle restart, ES went elevator down selling 65 points rapidly.
I wrote Tuesday at 4pm: “The obvious setup is the Failed Breakdown of todays low(s).
We went elevator down from 6950 to 6869 this morning.
I wrote yesterday “The obvious setup below here is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low at 6882.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a series of quality Failed Breakdowns (given to readers in advance) that we saw both before FOMC to fuel the 250 point initial leg up, and after FOMC.
6869 is an important support in its own right.” We saw exactly that this morning, selling to 6869 exact, recovering 6882, and squeezing to 6910.

post_title: SPX Cools-Off Slightly After A 7 Day Parabolic Rally. Is This All Bears Will Get Though? Oct 31 Plan
